    }\n497\t\n498\t        // Return parse result using serde_yaml for the actual value\n499\t        match serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content) {\n500\t            Ok(value) => {\n501\t                // Note: parse_errors from scope tracking are currently informational\n502\t                // They could be added as warnings in a future enhancement\n503\t                ParseResult::success(value)\n504\t            }\n505\t            Err(err) => {\n506\t                // Add any duplicate key errors we found to the parse error\n507\t                let error_msg = if parse_errors.is_empty() {\n508\t                    err.to_string()\n509\t                } else {\n510\t                    format!(\"{}; duplicate key errors: {}\", err, parse_errors.iter()\n511\t                        .map(|e| e.message.clone()).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\"; \"))\n512\t                };\n513\t                ParseResult::failure(ParseError::syntax(error_msg))\n514\t            }\n515\t        }\n516\t    }\n517\t\n518\t    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n519\t        // Convert bytes to string and parse\n520\t        match std::str::from_utf8(content) {\n521\t            Ok(utf8_content) => self.parse_str(utf8_content),\n522\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8: {}\", err))),\n523\t        }\n524\t    }\n525\t\n526\t    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n527\t        // Read file content\n528\t        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n529\t            Ok(content) => self.parse_str(&content),\n530\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Failed to read file {}: {}\", path.display(), err))),\n531\t        }\n532\t    }\n533\t\n534\t    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n535\t        // Create syntax validator based on parser mode\n536\t        let validator = if self.config.is_strict() {\n537\t            SyntaxValidator::strict()\n538\t        } else {\n539\t            SyntaxValidator::lenient()\n540\t        };\n541\t\n542\t        // Run syntax validation\n543\t        let mut result = validator.validate(content);\n544\t\n545\t        // Run scope-aware duplicate key detection (when duplicates are not allowed)\n546\t        if !self.config.allow_duplicates {\n547\t            let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n548\t            let duplicate_errors = self.detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(content, &mut scope_stack);\n549\t            if !duplicate_errors.is_empty() {\n550\t                result.valid = false;\n551\t                result.errors.extend(duplicate_errors);\n552\t            }\n553\t        }\n554\t\n555\t        // If no errors from basic validation, run enhanced detection\n556\t        if result.is_valid() {\n557\t            let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n558\t            let detector_result = detector.detect_to_validation_result(content);\n559\t\n560\t            // Merge errors from detector\n561\t            if !detector_result.is_valid() {\n562\t                result.valid = false;\n563\t                result.errors.extend(detector_result.errors);\n564\t            }\n565\t        }\n566\t\n567\t        result\n568\t    }\n569\t\n570\t    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult {\n571\t        // Read file content\n572\t        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n573\t            Ok(content) => content,\n574\t            Err(err) => {\n575\t                return ValidationResult {\n576\t                    valid: false,\n577\t                    errors: vec![ValidationError::new(\n578\t                        path.display().to_string(),\n579\t                        format!(\"failed to read file: {}\", err)\n580\t                    )],\n581\t                    warnings: Vec::new(),\n582\t                };\n583\t            }\n584\t        };\n585\t\n586\t        // Validate the content\n587\t        self.validate_str(&content)\n588\t    }\n589\t\n590\t    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig {\n591\t        &self.config\n592\t    }\n593\t\n594\t    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n595\t    where\n596\t        Self: Sized,\n597\t    {\n598\t        Self {\n599\t            config,\n600\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n601\t        }\n602\t    }\n603\t}\n604\t\n605\t// ============================================================================\n606\t// Integration Tests for Scope Tracking\n607\t// Core parser trait and implementations for YAML parsing\n//!\n//! This module defines the Parser trait and provides functionality for\n//! parsing YAML content from various sources.\n\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::{\n    types::{ParseResult, ValidationResult, ValidationError},\n    error::ParseError,\n    ParserConfig,\n    syntax_validator::SyntaxValidator,\n    syntax_detector::SyntaxDetector,\n    scope::ScopeStack,\n    line_parser::{calculate_indentation},\n    scope::extract_key_context,\n};\n\n#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\nuse log::debug as log_debug;\n#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\nuse log::warn as log_warn;\n\n/// Trait for YAML parsers\n///\n/// This trait defines the core interface for parsing YAML content.\n/// Implementations can parse from strings, files, or other sources.\npub trait Parser {\n    /// Parse YAML content from a string\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `content` - The YAML content as a string\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A ParseResult containing the parsed data or an error\n    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a byte slice\n    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a file\n    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Validate YAML content without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Validate a YAML file without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Get the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A reference to the parser's configuration\n    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig;\n\n    /// Set the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `config` - The new configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// The parser with the new configuration\n    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n    where\n        Self: Sized;\n}\n\n/// Basic YAML parser implementation\n///\n/// This is a minimal implementation of the Parser trait that\n/// provides basic YAML parsing functionality with scope-aware key tracking.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct BasicParser {\n    config: ParserConfig,\n    /// Scope stack for tracking keys within their proper scope contexts\n    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n}\n\nimpl BasicParser {\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with default configuration\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::default(),\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with the specified configuration\n    pub fn with_config(config: ParserConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new strict parser\n    ///\n    /// A strict parser enables strict mode and disallows duplicate keys.\n    /// Uses the comprehensive strict configuration from ParserConfig.\n    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::strict(),\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect duplicate keys using scope-aware tracking\n    ///\n    /// This method processes YAML content line-by-line, tracking keys within\n    /// their proper scope contexts. It returns a list of validation errors\n    /// for any duplicate keys found within the same scope.\n    fn detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(&self, content: &str, scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n        let mut duplicate_errors = Vec::new();\n\n        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n            let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n            // Skip empty lines and comments\n            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                {\n                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n                }\n                scope_stack.reset();\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip YAML directives\n            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n\n            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n                Ordering::Greater => {\n                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else {\n                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                            }\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n                        // - A scalar value continuation\n                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                        {\n                            log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Less => {\n                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}\",\n                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n\n                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Edge case: indent decreased but no key - we've already exited to the right scope\n                }\n                Ordering::Equal => {\n                    // Same scope - check for keys\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Sibling parent key: key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Edge case: same indent but no key - just continue, no scope change needed\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                        duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                            dup_err.message()\n                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    // Sequence item without a key context\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n                            indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        duplicate_errors\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for BasicParser {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Parser for BasicParser {\n    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Create a new scope stack for this parsing operation\n        let mut scope_stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n        let mut parse_errors = Vec::new();\n\n        // Parse line by line with scope-aware key tracking\n        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n            let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n            // Skip empty lines and comments for key tracking\n            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                {\n                    log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n                }\n                scope_stack.reset();\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip YAML directives\n            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n\n            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n                Ordering::Greater => {\n                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else {\n                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                            }\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n                        // - A scalar value continuation\n                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                        {\n                            log_debug!(\"[parse_str] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Less => {\n                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}\",\n                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n\n                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Equal => {\n                    // Same scope - check for keys\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Sibling parent key: key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n                    log_debug!(\"DEBUG [parse_str]: Entering sequence scope for key '{}' at indent {} on line {}\",\n                        ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                        parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                            dup_err.message()\n                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    // Sequence item without a key context\n                    log_debug!(\"DEBUG [parse_str]: Entering sequence scope (no key) at indent {} on line {}\",\n                        indent, line_num_1index);\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Return parse result using serde_yaml for the actual value\n        match serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content) {\n            Ok(value) => {\n                // Note: parse_errors from scope tracking are currently informational\n                // They could be added as warnings in a future enhancement\n                ParseResult::success(value)\n            }\n            Err(err) => {\n                // Add any duplicate key errors we found to the parse error\n                let error_msg = if parse_errors.is_empty() {\n                    err.to_string()\n                } else {\n                    format!(\"{}; duplicate key errors: {}\", err, parse_errors.iter()\n                        .map(|e| e.message.clone()).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\"; \"))\n                };\n                ParseResult::failure(ParseError::syntax(error_msg))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Convert bytes to string and parse\n        match std::str::from_utf8(content) {\n            Ok(utf8_content) => self.parse_str(utf8_content),\n            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8: {}\", err))),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Read file content\n        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n            Ok(content) => self.parse_str(&content),\n            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Failed to read file {}: {}\", path.display(), err))),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n        // Create syntax validator based on parser mode\n        let validator = if self.config.is_strict() {\n            SyntaxValidator::strict()\n        } else {\n            SyntaxValidator::lenient()\n        };\n\n        // Run syntax validation\n        let mut result = validator.validate(content);\n\n        // Run scope-aware duplicate key detection (when duplicates are not allowed)\n        if !self.config.allow_duplicates {\n            let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n            let duplicate_errors = self.detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(content, &mut scope_stack);\n            if !duplicate_errors.is_empty() {\n                result.valid = false;\n                result.errors.extend(duplicate_errors);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // If no errors from basic validation, run enhanced detection\n        if result.is_valid() {\n            let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n            let detector_result = detector.detect_to_validation_result(content);\n\n            // Merge errors from detector\n            if !detector_result.is_valid() {\n                result.valid = false;\n                result.errors.extend(detector_result.errors);\n            }\n        }\n\n        result\n    }\n\n    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult {\n        // Read file content\n        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n            Ok(content) => content,\n            Err(err) => {\n                return ValidationResult {\n                    valid: false,\n                    errors: vec![ValidationError::new(\n                        path.display().to_string(),\n                        format!(\"failed to read file: {}\", err)\n                    )],\n                    warnings: Vec::new(),\n                };\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Validate the content\n        self.validate_str(&content)\n    }\n\n    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig {\n        &self.config\n    }\n\n    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n    where\n        Self: Sized,\n    {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Integration Tests for Scope Tracking\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod integration_tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    /// Test parse_str with nested YAML structures\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_str_with_nested_yaml() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n    port: 8080\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com\n    port: 5432\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should parse valid nested YAML successfully\");\n\n        // Verify we can access the parsed structure\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        assert!(value.is_mapping());\n        assert!(value.get(\"services\").is_some());\n    }\n\n    /// Test parse_str with deeply nested YAML\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_str_with_deeply_nested_yaml() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\napplication:\n  server:\n    config:\n      timeouts:\n        connect: 30\n        read: 60\n    logging:\n      level: info\n  database:\n    connection:\n      pool:\n        max: 10\n        min: 2\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should parse deeply nested YAML successfully\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that same key in different scopes passes\n    #[test]\n    fn test_same_key_different_scopes_passes() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        // This YAML has 'host' and 'port' in different scopes\n        let yaml = r#\"\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n    port: 8080\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com\n    port: 5432\n  cache:\n    host: redis.example.com\n    port: 6379\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Same key in different scopes should be allowed\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        let services = &value[\"services\"];\n        assert_eq!(services[\"web\"][\"host\"], \"localhost\");\n        assert_eq!(services[\"database\"][\"host\"], \"db.example.com\");\n        assert_eq!(services[\"cache\"][\"host\"], \"redis.example.com\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that duplicate key in same scope is detected by strict parser\n    #[test]\n    fn test_duplicate_key_same_scope_detected() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::strict(); // Strict parser doesn't allow duplicates\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nconfig:\n  server:\n    host: localhost\n    host: duplicate-value\n    port: 8080\n\"#;\n\n        // The parse_str itself may succeed (serde_yaml's behavior), but validation should detect the duplicate\n        let validation_result = parser.validate_str(yaml);\n        assert!(!validation_result.is_valid(), \"Validation should detect duplicate keys\");\n\n        // Check that error message mentions duplicate key\n        let error_messages: Vec<String> = validation_result.errors\n            .iter()\n            .map(|e| e.message.clone())\n            .collect();\n        assert!(error_messages.iter().any(|msg| msg.contains(\"duplicate\") || msg.contains(\"host\")));\n    }\n\n    /// Test parse_str with sequence items having same keys\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_str_sequence_items_same_keys() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nitems:\n  - name: item1\n    value: 100\n  - name: item2\n    value: 200\n  - name: item3\n    value: 300\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Sequence items with same keys should be allowed\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        let items = {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_5d42ac956ce44c159762d444","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Scope representation data structures\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module defines the core data structures for representing hierarchical scopes\n4\t//! in YAML documents. These structures enable accurate duplicate key detection by\n5\t//! tracking keys within their proper scope contexts.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! # Overview\n8\t//!\n9\t//! YAML documents use indentation to define nested scopes. Keys in different scopes\n10\t//! can have the same name without being duplicates. For example:\n11\t//!\n12\t//! ```yaml\n13\t//! services:\n14\t//!   web:\n15\t//!     host: localhost    # \"host\" in services.web scope\n16\t//!     port: 8080\n17\t//!   database:\n18\t//!     host: db.example.com  # \"host\" in services.database scope (NOT a duplicate)\n19\t//!     port: 5432\n20\t//! ```\n21\t//!\n22\t//! The scope representation system tracks:\n23\t//! - **Scope**: A mapping context at a specific nesting level\n24\t//! - **ScopeStack**: A hierarchical stack of active scopes during parsing\n25\t//! - **KeyContext**: Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n26\t//!\n27\t//! # Architecture\n28\t//!\n29\t//! ## Scope\n30\t//!\n31\t//! A `Scope` represents a single mapping context with:\n32\t//! - Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n33\t//! - Set of keys defined within this scope\n34\t//! - Line number where this scope started\n35\t//! - Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n36\t//! - Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n37\t//!\n38\t//! ## ScopeStack\n39\t//!\n40\t//! A `ScopeStack` manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes:\n41\t//! - Maintains a stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n42\t//! - Tracks base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n43\t//! - Handles scope transitions (enter/exit) as indentation changes\n44\t//! - Provides duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts\n45\t//!\n46\t//! # Examples\n47\t//!\n48\t//! ```\n49\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack};\n50\t//!\n51\t//! let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\n52\t//!\n53\t//! // Enter a scope when encountering a parent mapping\n54\t//! stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n55\t//!\n56\t//! // Add keys to current scope\n57\t//! stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n58\t//! stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n59\t//!\n60\t//! // Detect duplicate in same scope\n61\t//! let result = stack.add_key(\"web\", 4);\n62\t//! assert!(result.is_err()); // Duplicate key error\n63\t//! ```\n64\t\n65\tuse std::collections::HashSet;\n66\tuse std::fmt;\n67\t\n68\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n69\tuse log::debug as log_debug;\n70\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n71\tuse log::warn as log_warn;\n72\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n73\tuse log::trace as log_trace;\n74\t\n75\t/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n76\t///\n77\t/// Scopes are created when the parser encounters a parent mapping (a key whose value\n78\t/// is itself a mapping). Each scope maintains its own set of keys, independent of\n79\t/// keys in parent or sibling scopes.\n80\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n81\tpub struct Scope {\n82\t    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n83\t    pub indent_level: usize,\n84\t    /// Keys defined within this scope\n85\t    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\n86\t    /// Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n87\t    pub start_line: usize,\n88\t    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n89\t    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\n90\t    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n91\t    pub is_flow_style: bool,\n92\t    /// Whether this scope is within a sequence context\n93\t    pub in_sequence_context: bool,\n94\t    /// Unique identifier for sequence items to distinguish them at same indent\n95\t    pub sequence_item_id: Option<usize>,\n96\t}\n97\t\n98\timpl Scope {\n99\t    /// Create a new scope\n100\t    ///\n101\t    /// # Arguments\n102\t    ///\n103\t    /// * `indent_level` - Number of leading spaces for this scope\n104\t    /// * `start_line` - Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n105\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n106\t    ///\n107\t    /// # Examples\n108\t    ///\n109\t    /// ```\n110\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n111\t    ///\n112\t    /// let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n113\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 2);\n114\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 5);\n115\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n116\t    /// ```\n117\t    pub fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n118\t        Self {\n119\t            indent_level,\n120\t            keys: HashSet::new(),\n121\t            start_line,\n122\t            parent_key,\n123\t            is_flow_style: false,\n124\t            in_sequence_context: false,\n125\t            sequence_item_id: None,\n126\t        }\n127\t    }\n128\t\n129\t    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n130\t    ///\n131\t    /// # Arguments\n132\t    ///\n133\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\n134\t    ///\n135\t    /// # Returns\n136\t    ///\n137\t    /// `true` if the key already exists in this scope (duplicate), `false` otherwise\n138\t    ///\n139\t    /// # Examples\n140\t    ///\n141\t    /// ```\n142\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n143\t    ///\n144\t    /// let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n145\t    ///\n146\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), false); // First occurrence\n147\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"second\"), false); // Different key\n148\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), true);  // Duplicate!\n149\t    /// ```\n150\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n151\t        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n152\t    }\n153\t\n154\t    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n155\t    ///\n156\t    /// # Arguments\n157\t    ///\n158\t    /// * `key` - The key to check\n159\t    ///\n160\t    /// # Returns\n161\t    ///\n162\t    /// `true` if the key exists in this scope, `false` otherwise\n163\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n164\t        self.keys.contains(key)\n165\t    }\n166\t\n167\t    /// Get the number of keys in this scope\n168\t    pub fn key_count(&self) -> usize {\n169\t        self.keys.len()\n170\t    }\n171\t\n172\t    /// Clear all keys from this scope\n173\t    pub fn clear_keys(&mut self) {\n174\t        self.keys.clear();\n175\t    }\n176\t}\n177\t\n178\timpl fmt::Display for Scope {\n179\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n180\t        write!(f, \"Scope(indent={}, \", self.indent_level)?;\n181\t        if let Some(ref parent) = self.parent_key {\n182\t            write!(f, \"parent={}, \", parent)?;\n183\t        }\n184\t        write!(f, \"keys={})\", self.keys.len())\n185\t    }\n186\t}\n187\t\n188\t/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n189\t///\n190\t/// The ScopeStack maintains the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes during parsing.\n191\t/// As the parser encounters different indentation levels, it enters and exits scopes,\n192\t/// ensuring that duplicate key detection only considers keys within the same scope.\n193\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n194\tpub struct ScopeStack {\n195\t    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n196\t    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n197\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n198\t    base_indent: usize,\n199\t    /// Sequence item counter for generating unique IDs\n200\t    sequence_item_counter: usize,\n201\t}\n202\t\n203\timpl ScopeStack {\n204\t    /// Create a new scope stack\n205\t    ///\n206\t    /// # Arguments\n207\t    ///\n208\t    /// * `base_indent` - The base indentation size in spaces (usually 2 or 4)\n209\t    ///\n210\t    /// # Examples\n211\t    ///\n212\t    /// ```\n213\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n214\t    ///\n215\t    /// let stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // 2-space indentation\n216\t    /// ```\n217\t    pub fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n218\t        Self {\n219\t            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n220\t            base_indent,\n221\t            sequence_item_counter: 0,\n222\t        }\n223\t    }\n224\t\n225\t    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n226\t    ///\n227\t    /// # Panics\n228\t    ///\n229\t    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty (should never happen in normal operation)\n230\t    pub fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n231\t        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n232\t    }\n233\t\n234\t    /// Get the current scope as an immutable reference\n235\t    ///\n236\t    /// # Panics\n237\t    ///\n238\t    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty\n239\t    pub fn current_scope_ref(&self) -> &Scope {\n240\t        self.scopes.last().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n241\t    }\n242\t\n243\t    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n244\t    ///\n245\t    /// # Arguments\n246\t    ///\n247\t    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level to search for\n248\t    ///\n249\t    /// # Returns\n250\t    ///\n251\t    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a scope at this level exists, `None` otherwise\n252\t    pub fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n253\t        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n254\t    }\n255\t\n256\t    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n257\t    ///\n258\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters increased indentation,\n259\t    /// indicating entry into a nested scope.\n260\t    ///\n261\t    /// # Arguments\n262\t    ///\n263\t    /// * `indent_level` - The new indentation level\n264\t    /// * `line` - The line number where this scope starts (1-indexed)\n265\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n266\t    ///\n267\t    /// # Examples\n268\t    ///\n269\t    /// ```\n270\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n271\t    ///\n272\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n273\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n274\t    /// ```\n275\t    pub fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n276\t        // Debug logging for scope entry\n277\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n278\t        {\n279\t            let parent_info = parent_key.as_ref().map(|k| k.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"<anonymous>\");\n280\t            let scope_type = \"Mapping\";\n281\t            log_debug!(\n282\t                \"[SCOPE ENTRY] type={}, line={}, indent={}, parent='{}', current_depth={}, path='{}'\",\n283\t                scope_type,\n284\t                line,\n285\t                indent_level,\n286\t                parent_info,\n287\t                self.depth(),\n288\t                self.get_scope_path()\n289\t            );\n290\t        }\n291\t\n292\t        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n293\t        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n294\t            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\n295\t            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\n296\t            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n297\t            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\n298\t\n299\t            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n300\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= indent_level);\n301\t            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\n302\t\n303\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n304\t            {\n305\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (reuse), indent={}, cleared scopes deeper than this level\", indent_level);\n306\t            }\n307\t        } else {\n308\t            // Create new scope\n309\t            let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n310\t            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n311\t\n312\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n313\t            {\n314\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (new), indent={}, created new scope at this level\", indent_level);\n315\t            }\n316\t        }\n317\t\n318\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n319\t        {\n320\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] After entry: depth={}, current_indent={}\", self.depth(), self.current_indent());\n321\t        }\n322\t    }\n323\t\n324\t    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n325\t    ///\n326\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters decreased indentation,\n327\t    /// indicating exit from a nested scope back to its parent.\n328\t    ///\n329\t    /// # Arguments\n330\t    ///\n331\t    /// * `target_indent` - The indentation level to exit to\n332\t    ///\n333\t    /// # Examples\n334\t    ///\n335\t    /// ```\n336\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n337\t    ///\n338\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n339\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n340\t    /// stack.exit_to_scope(0); // Exit back to root\n341\t    /// ```\n342\t    pub fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n343\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n344\t        {\n345\t            log_debug!(\n346\t                \"[SCOPE EXIT] target_indent={}, current_depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\",\n347\t                target_indent,\n348\t                self.depth(),\n349\t                self.current_indent(),\n350\t                self.get_scope_path()\n351\t            );\n352\t        }\n353\t\n354\t        // Edge case: can't exit to a deeper level than current\n355\t        if target_indent > self.current_indent() {\n356\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n357\t            {\n358\t                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: target_indent={} > current_indent={}, ignoring exit request\", target_indent, self.current_indent());\n359\t            }\n360\t            return;\n361\t        }\n362\t\n363\t        // Edge case: can't exit if stack would become empty\n364\t        let would_be_empty = self.scopes.iter()\n365\t            .filter(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent)\n366\t            .count() == 0;\n367\t\n368\t        if would_be_empty {\n369\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n370\t            {\n371\t                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: exit would empty scope stack, keeping at least root scope\");\n372\t            }\n373\t            // Keep at least the root scope\n374\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level == 0);\n375\t            return;\n376\t        }\n377\t\n378\t        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n379\t        let before_depth = self.depth();\n380\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n381\t\n382\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n383\t        {\n384\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] Removed {} scopes deeper than indent={}\", before_depth - self.depth(), target_indent);\n385\t        }\n386\t\n387\t        // Ensure we have a scope at the target level\n388\t        // Check if there's a scope at target_indent + base_indent (for parent key scopes)\n389\t        let adjusted_target = target_indent + self.base_indent;\n390\t        if !self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == target_indent) {\n391\t            // Check if there's a scope at the adjusted level (parent key scope)\n392\t            if self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == adjusted_target) {\n393\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n394\t                {\n395\t                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] Found scope at adjusted indent={}, no fallback needed\", adjusted_target);\n396\t                }\n397\t                // Scope exists at adjusted level, no need for fallback\n398\t            } else {\n399\t                // This shouldn't happen in valid YAML, but handle gracefully\n400\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n401\t                {\n402\t                    log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: No scope found at indent={} or adjusted_indent={}, creating fallback scope at target_indent\", target_indent, adjusted_target);\n403\t                }\n404\t                let fallback_scope = Scope::new(target_indent, 0, None);\n405\t                self.scopes.push(fallback_scope);\n406\t            }\n407\t        }\n408\t\n409\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n410\t        {\n411\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] After exit: depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\", self.depth(), self.current_indent(), self.get_scope_path());\n412\t        }\n413\t    }\n414\t\n415\t    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n416\t    ///\n417\t    /// # Arguments\n418\t    ///\n419\t    /// * `key` - The key to check for\n420\t    ///\n421\t    /// # Returns\n422\t    ///\n423\t    /// `true` if the key exists in the current scope, `false` otherwise\n424\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n425\t        self.scopes.last()\n426\t            .and_then(|scope| Some(scope.contains_key(key)))\n427\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n428\t    }\n429\t\n430\t    /// Check if any scope in the hierarchy contains a key\n431\t    ///\n432\t    /// This searches through all scopes in the stack, not just the current one.\n433\t    ///\n434\t    /// # Arguments\n435\t    ///\n436\t    /// * `key` - The key to search for\n437\t    ///\n438\t    /// # Returns\n439\t    ///\n440\t    /// `true` if the key exists in any scope, `false` otherwise\n441\t    pub fn contains_key_in_any_scope(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n442\t        self.scopes.iter().any(|scope| scope.contains_key(key))\n443\t    }\n444\t\n445\t    /// Add a key to current scope\n446\t    ///\n447\t    /// # Arguments\n448\t    ///\n449\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\n450\t    /// * `line` - The line number where the key appears (1-indexed)\n451\t    ///\n452\t    /// # Returns\n453\t    ///\n454\t    /// `Ok(())` if the key was added successfully\n455\t    /// `Err(DuplicateKeyError)` if the key already exists in the current scope\n456\t    ///\n457\t    /// # Examples\n458\t    ///\n459\t    /// ```\n460\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n461\t    ///\n462\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n463\t    ///\n464\t    /// // First occurrence succeeds\n465\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).is_ok());\n466\t    ///\n467\t    /// // Second occurrence fails\n468\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).is_err());\n469\t    /// ```\n470\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError> {\n471\t        let scope_path = self.get_scope_path();\n472\t\n473\t        // Check if key exists before adding\n474\t        if self.contains_key(key) {\n475\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n476\t            Err(DuplicateKeyError {\n477\t                key: key.to_string(),\n478\t                scope_path,\n479\t                first_line: scope.start_line,\n480\t                duplicate_line: line,\n481\t            })\n482\t        } else {\n483\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n484\t            scope.add_key(key);\n485\t            Ok(())\n486\t        }\n487\t    }\n488\t\n489\t    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n490\t    ///\n491\t    /// Returns a dot-separated path representing the scope hierarchy,\n492\t    /// e.g., \"services.web.database\" for a deeply nested scope.\n493\t    ///\n494\t    /// # Returns\n495\t    ///\n496\t    /// A string representing the scope path\n497\t    ///\n498\t    /// # Examples\n499\t    ///\n500\t    /// ```\n501\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n502\t    ///\n503\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n504\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n505\t    /// stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n506\t    /// assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"services.web\");\n507\t    /// ```\n508\t    pub fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n509\t        let mut path = Vec::new();\n510\t        for scope in &self.scopes {\n511\t            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n512\t                path.push(key.clone());\n513\t            }\n514\t        }\n515\t        path.join(\".\")\n516\t    }\n517\t\n518\t    /// Get current indent level\n519\t    ///\n520\t    /// # Returns\n521\t    ///\n522\t    /// The indentation level of the current scope\n523\t    pub fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n524\t        self.scopes.last()\n525\t            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n526\t            .unwrap_or(0)\n527\t    }\n528\t\n529\t    /// Get the number of active scopes in the stack\n530\t    pub fn depth(&self) -> usize {\n531\t        self.scopes.len()\n532\t    }\n533\t\n534\t    /// Clear all scopes and reset to root\n535\t    ///\n536\t    /// This resets the scope stack to its initial state with only the root scope.\n537\t    pub fn reset(&mut self) {\n538\t        self.scopes = vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)];\n539\t    }\n540\t\n541\t    /// Get the base indentation size\n542\t    pub fn base_indent(&self) -> usize {\n543\t        self.base_indent\n544\t    }\n545\t\n546\t    /// Set the base indentation size\n547\t    ///\n548\t    /// # Arguments\n549\t    ///\n550\t    /// * `base_indent` - The new base indentation size in spaces\n551\t    pub fn set_base_indent(&mut self, base_indent: usize) {\n552\t        self.base_indent = base_indent;\n553\t    }\n554\t\n555\t    /// Enter a sequence context (when we see a `-` item)\n556\t    ///\n557\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters a sequence item,\n558\t    /// creating a new scope for that item to prevent false duplicate detection\n559\t    /// between items at the same indentation level.\n560\t    ///\n561\t    /// # Arguments\n562\t    ///\n563\t    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level of the sequence item\n564\t    /// * `line` - The line number where this sequence item starts (1-indexed)\n565\t    ///\n566\t    /// # Examples\n567\t    ///\n568\t    /// ```\n569\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n570\t    ///\n571\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n572\t    /// stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5); // Enter sequence item at indent 2\n573\t    /// ```\n574\t    pub fn enter_sequence_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize) {\n575\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n576\t        {\n577\t            log_debug!(\n578\t                \"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, line={}, indent={}, current_depth={}, path='{}'\",\n579\t                line,\n580\t                indent_level,\n581\t                self.depth(),\n582\t                self.get_scope_path()\n583\t            );\n584\t        }\n585\t\n586\t        // Remove all scopes deeper than this level, but preserve parent mapping scopes\n587\t        // When entering a sequence scope, we need to keep the parent mapping scope intact\n588\t        // while clearing any nested scopes from previous content\n589\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level < indent_level || s.parent_key.is_some());\n590\t\n591\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n592\t        {\n593\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, indent={}, cleared scopes deeper than this level, preserved parent mappings\", indent_level);\n594\t        }\n595\t\n596\t        // Check if there's already a scope at this level that's in a sequence context\n597\t        let needs_new_scope = self.scopes.last()\n598\t            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level != indent_level || !scope.in_sequence_context)\n599\t            .unwrap_or(true);\n600\t\n601\t        if needs_new_scope {\n602\t            // Create a new scope for this sequence item\n603\t            let mut new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, None);\n604\t            new_scope.in_sequence_context = true;\n605\t            self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n606\t            new_scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n607\t            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n608\t\n609\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n610\t            {\n611\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence (new), item_id={}, created new sequence scope\", self.sequence_item_counter);\n612\t            }\n613\t        } else {\n614\t            // Reset the existing scope for a new sequence item\n615\t            if let Some(scope) = self.scopes.last_mut() {\n616\t                scope.keys.clear();\n617\t                scope.start_line = line;\n618\t                self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n619\t                scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n620\t\n621\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n622\t                {\n623\t                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence (reset), item_id={}, reset existing sequence scope\", self.sequence_item_counter);\n624\t                }\n625\t            }\n626\t        }\n627\t\n628\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n629\t        {\n630\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, after entry: depth={}, in_sequence={}\", self.depth(), self.in_sequence_context());\n631\t        }\n632\t    }\n633\t\n634\t    /// Check if we're in a sequence context\n635\t    ///\n636\t    /// # Returns\n637\t    ///\n638\t    /// `true` if the current scope is within a sequence context, `false` otherwise\n639\t    pub fn in_sequence_context(&self) -> bool {\n640\t        self.scopes.last()\n641\t            .map(|scope| scope.in_sequence_context)\n642\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n643\t    }\n644\t}\n645\t\n646\timpl fmt::Display for ScopeStack {\n647\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n648\t        write!(f, \"ScopeStack(depth={}, base_indent={}, current_path={})\",\n649\t               self.depth(), self.base_indent, self.get_scope_path())\n650\t    }\n651\t}\n652\t\n653\t/// Error when a duplicate key    /// Create a new duplicate key error\n671\t    ///\n672\t    /// # Arguments\n673\t    ///\n674\t    /// * `key` - The duplicate key name\n675\t    /// * `scope_path` - The scope path where the
